channel.title: NYT > Arts > Television
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: On ‘Company Retreat,’ Anthony Norman Finished the Job
item.description: The unwitting hero of this Amazon prank comedy series acted heroically in the final episode.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski and Jamie Lee Taete
item.category: Jury Duty Presents: Company Retreat (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Jamie Lee Taete for The New York Times

item.title: Danish Shows to Watch While We’re Still at Peace
item.description: The protector of Greenland also makes very entertaining TV.
item.dc:creator: Mike Hale

item.title: ‘Hacks’ Has the Last Laugh
item.description: As the HBO comedy finished production earlier this year, a photographer captured some of the final days on set and the showrunners looked back on its award-winning run.
item.dc:creator: Chantal Anderson and Alexis Soloski

item.title: ‘The Pitt’ Season 2 Episode 13 Recap: Should He Stay or Should He Go?
item.description: Dr. Robby finally says what his colleagues had been thinking.
item.dc:creator: Sean T. Collins

item.title: Sarah Shahi Plays a Therapist on ‘Paradise’ and Nearly Everywhere Else
item.description: The actress has gotten used to dispensing advice, including on this Hulu drama and in a new self-help memoir.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Spencer and Magdalena Wosinska
item.media:credit: Magdalena Wosinska for The New York Times

item.title: Taylor Frankie Paul’s Past Was No Secret. A Child’s Cry Changed Everything.
item.description: Before being cast as “The Bachelorette,” Taylor Frankie Paul had discussed — with police, on podcasts and on TV — the domestic dispute that involved her 5-year-old daughter.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and John Koblin
item.category: The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: ‘For All Mankind’ Is the Anti-‘Black Mirror’
item.description: The optimistic space-race drama, starting its fifth season, imagines humans settling space without tech colonizing our minds.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik

item.title: How ‘Love Story’ Gave Viewers a History Lesson on the Kennedys and ’90s NYC
item.description: The FX show, which dramatizes the relationship between John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette, has inspired a legion of younger viewers to dig into the couple’s past and 1990s New York City.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.category: Love Story: John F Kennedy Jr & Carolyn Bessette (TV Program)

item.title: Don’t Let the Name Harry Hole Fool You. The Important One Is Jo Nesbo.
item.description: After a disappointing movie adaptation, the Norwegian author took the reins as showrunner in a new Netflix series based on his Detective Hole books.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Vincentelli
item.media:credit: David B. Torch for The New York Times
item.media:description: The actor Tobias Santelmann, left, stars at the title character in the writer Jo Nesbo’s “Detective Hole,” a new series-length Netflix adaptation, which Nesbo oversaw.

item.title: Regé-Jean Page on the Album He Considers Flawless
item.description: “It’s raw, real, sensitive, strong, vulnerable, righteous, romantic, genius,” said the “Bridgerton” actor, who stars in the new film “You, Me & Tuscany.”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

item.title: What to Know About ‘The Testaments,’ a Sequel to Margaret Atwood’s ‘The Handmaid’s Tale’
item.description: “The Testaments” focuses on a younger generation coming of age inside Gilead, the religious regime first imagined in Margaret Atwood’s 1985 dystopian thriller.
item.dc:creator: Martine Thompson
item.category: The Testaments: The Sequel to The Handmaid's Tale (Book)

item.title: Late Night Says Goodbye to Pam Bondi
item.description: President Trump is “on a bit of a firing spree,” Jimmy Fallon said on “The Tonight Show” on Thursday, adding, “Ironically, the only staffer who has immunity is RFK Jr.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Why Everyone Loves ‘Love on the Spectrum’
item.description: Without exploitation, “Love on the Spectrum” captures the triumphs and travails of dating. It has become one of Netflix’s most popular shows.
item.dc:creator: Anna Peele
item.media:credit: Ryan Pfluger for The New York Times
item.media:description: Madison Marilla and Tyler White, two stars of “Love on the Spectrum.”

item.title: Desi Lydic Wants Trump to Take a Hint About the Ballroom
item.description: “Might I suggest: If the ballroom starts experiencing ballistic assault, maybe we just cancel the ball?” she said on “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Eugene Mirman, ‘Bob’s Burgers’ Voice Actor, Seriously Injured in Car Crash
item.description: Mr. Mirman crashed his car on Tuesday in New Hampshire. He remains hospitalized with serious but non-life-threatening injuries.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.media:description: Eugene Mirman, a comedian and actor known for his role as Gene Belcher on the animated series “Bob’s Burgers,” was hospitalized on Tuesday with serious injuries after crashing his car in New Hampshire.

item.title: Bravo’s ‘Summer House’ West Wilson and Amanda Batula Scandal, Explained
item.description: Will the newly announced “connection” between the co-stars West Wilson and Amanda Batula become Scandoval 2.0?
item.dc:creator: Jessica Roy
item.media:description: Amanda Batula and West Wilson released a joint statement on Tuesday saying that their “connection grew out of a genuine, longstanding friendship.”

item.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows Coming to Netflix in April
item.description: New shows of note this month include a new season of “Beef” and an animated “Stranger Things” spinoff.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.media:description: Oscar Isaac and Carey Mulligan play an unhappy couple in the second season of “Beef,” premiering on April 16.

item.title: When Stars’ Plastic Surgery Is Played for Your Entertainment
item.description: On TikTok and Instagram, content creators play detective, trying to figure out what work has been done. But they’re really just critiquing women’s looks.
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman

item.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows Streaming in April 2026
item.description: A new Jonathan Glatzer tech satire and an adaptation of Isabel Allende’s novel “The House of the Spirits” are among this month’s highlights.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.category: The House of the Spirits (TV Program)
Star Wars: Maul - Shadow Lord (TV Program)

item.title: Stephen Colbert Blames ‘The Bachelorette’ Cancellation for $4 Gas
item.description: The “Late Show” host joked that “Trump attacked Iran” after ABC scrapped the reality show’s latest season.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: ‘The Book of Mormon’ Is Sorry if You Were Offended for 15 Years
item.description: The taboo-busting, gasp-inducing Broadway musical comedy has been a hit with audiences and critics. But could it be produced today?
item.dc:creator: Jesse Green and Brian Karlsson
item.category: Mormons (Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints)
item.media:description: When “The Book of Mormon” opened in 2011, Matt Stone, left, Trey Parker, center, and Robert Lopez were worried that their creation would flop.

item.title: Stephen Colbert Marvels at ‘No Kings’ Rallies Worldwide
item.description: “Just look at the crowd we got here in New York,” he said. “Oop, that’s the T.S.A. line at J.F.K.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)

item.title: ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ on Broadway, With Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, Is Underbaked
item.description: A raucous adaptation of a gritty portrait of New York stifles tension with comedy, leaving its stars, Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach, adrift.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman
item.media:description: Jon Bernthal, center, as Sonny, with Jessica Hecht as the mouthy bank teller Colleen and Danny Johnson as a security guard in “Dog Day Afternoon” at the August Wilson Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: In Her Broadway Debut, Mariska Hargitay Will Replace Daniel Radcliffe in ‘Every Brilliant Thing’
item.description: Starting in May, Hargitay will make her Broadway debut in “Every Brilliant Thing,” an elastic play that shape shifts to fit a distinctly different star.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.category: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit (TV Program)
item.media:description: “We wanted somebody going in a completely different direction,” one of the directors said about the actress Mariska Hargitay, who is the long-running star of “Law & Order: Special Victims Unit.”

item.title: In a New Memoir, Arsenio Hall Recalls His High-Flying Years as a Talk-Show Host
item.description: Eddie Murphy, Snoop Dogg and Bill Clinton (naturally) show up in his gossipy new memoir. He isn’t very sentimental.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman
item.media:credit: Erik Carter for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hosting a hit talk show “allowed me to fantasize, to be the guy I wanted to be,” Arsenio Hall says.

item.title: ‘The Real Housewives of Rhode Island,’ Plus 7 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new iteration of the Bravo franchise begins and the second season of “Your Friends & Neighbors” premieres.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez
item.media:description: A still from “The Real Housewives of Rhode Island.”

item.title: ‘The Comeback’ Season 3, Episode 2: Getting the Band Back Together
item.description: Valerie doesn’t fully understand why people in Hollywood are so worked up about A.I. She just sees a wave she thinks she knows how to ride.
item.dc:creator: Noel Murray
item.media:description: The director James Burrows, as himself, with Lisa Kudrow, as Valerie Cherish, in a scene from “The Comeback.”

item.title: Eric Overmyer, Who Wrote for Modern Television Classics, Dies at 74
item.description: Trained as a playwright, he got his first TV writing job on “St. Elsewhere,” then worked on “Homicide: Life on the Street,” “The Wire,” “Treme” and “Bosch.”
item.dc:creator: Richard Sandomir
item.category: Homicide: Life on the Street (TV Program)
item.media:description: Eric Overmyer in 1994. “His scripts have a remarkable ability to thrill, enlighten and bemuse,” the writer and television producer Tom Fontana said.
